data.title: As private lending recedes, Bitcoin will become a safe-haven winner.
data.description:
Author: Jordi Visser, Senior Analyst on Wall Street; Translated by: Shaw Jinse Finance

The next major Bitcoin bull market may start from the most unexpected and seemingly unrelated area: the private lending market.

This is not because a collapse in private lending would immediately benefit Bitcoin. Not at all. In a true liquidity crisis, highly liquid assets like Bitcoin are usually the first to be sold off along with other assets.The first phase is not liberation, but liquidation. The second phase is where the core logic truly takes effect. In a system so heavily indebted, so deeply financialized, and politically intolerant of prolonged credit contraction, the "ebb tide" of liquidity almost never lasts long.

When official liquidity is reinjected, Bitcoin will grasp the implications of this action almost faster than any other asset. Warren Buffett famously described this: "Only when the tide goes out do you discover who's been swimming naked." He also mocked private equity's "cherished fee structures and obsession with leverage," later warning that in certain rare moments, "credit will disappear, and debt will become a deadly financial trap." Buffett wasn't referring to Bitcoin at the time, but rather diagnosing a system built on leverage, opacity, and confidence. This diagnosis applies perfectly to private lending today. When the tide goes out, hidden vulnerabilities will no longer be theoretical risks, but the entire theme of the market. This is precisely why private lending is so important today. Morgan Stanley estimates the market size at approximately $3 trillion in early 2025, potentially approaching $5 trillion by 2029. The initial warning signs have already appeared. This week, Morgan Stanley imposed redemption restrictions on one of its private credit funds after investors attempted to redeem nearly 11% of its outstanding shares; meanwhile, JPMorgan Chase made impairment provisions for some loans invested in private credit funds due to increasingly stringent market scrutiny of software-related exposures. The key point is not that the entire market is in crisis, but that the pressure is no longer hypothetical. The pressure is now manifesting in redemptions, asset impairment, and the behavior of lending institutions. Artificial intelligence is the catalyst. The core vulnerability lies not only in leverage, but in the fact that leverage is tied to an industry whose business model is being repriced in real time. Morgan Stanley stated in March that approximately 25% of its Business Development Corporation (BDC) portfolio was allocated to the software sector. This concentration is extremely high, given that artificial intelligence (AI) is reshaping the business models of the software industry. For years, the financing logic of the software industry has been based on the assumption that recurring revenue means stable cash flow, high customer stickiness, high profit margins, and a reliable exit strategy. AI is challenging every aspect of this assumption: **pricing power is being squeezed, products are becoming features more quickly, competitive moats are narrowing, and new computing power and product investment are becoming necessary.** In other words, **a large amount of private lending is based on a software industry model that may be disappearing.** Bitcoin is also caught in the same headwinds. **All discussions surrounding software valuation and private lending ultimately circle back to Bitcoin.** When you look at this chart that overlays Bitcoin, software stocks, and private equity stock prices, the correlation is immediately apparent. **Bitcoin's price movement exhibits the dual characteristics of software stock beta and liquidity beta.** And currently, both of these forces are simultaneously suppressing it. As we entered 2025, I anticipated a strong surge in Bitcoin's price. Driving factors included increased government support and the rise of AI agents—both of which should have amplified the network effects of cryptocurrencies, propelling them into the ranks of high-growth asset classes alongside the software industry's revaluation. Despite increases in stablecoin trading volume and market capitalization, this optimistic outlook for Bitcoin has failed to materialize. Instead, with the rollout of technologies like Opus 4.5 and OpenClaw, the market focus shifted to autonomous AI, and the mainstream narrative evolved into AI's disruptive reshaping of the software industry. Investors are reassessing the sustainability of traditional software business models, leading to a rapid revaluation of valuation multiples. This revaluation is also putting pressure on private lending—a core source of funding for the software ecosystem. When AI forces a disruptive revaluation of the software industry, a key macroeconomic attribute of Bitcoin comes under pressure. Simultaneously, a tightening global liquidity cycle impacts another core characteristic of Bitcoin—its high sensitivity to global liquidity. This is why cracks in private lending don't immediately benefit Bitcoin. In the short term, the opposite is often true. Bitcoin is highly liquid, diversified, and easy to sell. In the first phase of market stress, the impact of liquidity attributes far outweighs long-term investment logic. Bitcoin doesn't trigger panic first; instead, it acts as a "rescue" force before the market. History has proven this logical sequence. During the March 2020 "cash crunch," Reuters reported that Bitcoin plummeted by over 20% in a single day, with a cumulative drop of over 30% in five days—investors sold off almost all assets. Subsequently, global monetary easing began. By January 2021, Bitcoin had risen over 900% from its March lows. Governments worldwide increased spending to cushion the impact of the pandemic, and investors worried about inflation and currency devaluation. Bitcoin did not ignore the panic, but its pricing reaction to the "rescue policies" was more dramatic than most assets. The same pattern repeated itself in the 2023 US regional banking crisis. A review by the Federal Reserve and the Inspector General revealed that Silicon Valley Bank (SVB) customers withdrew $42 billion in a single day, with another $100 billion in withdrawal requests queuing up the following day. Subsequently, authorities provided guarantees for all depositors, and the Federal Reserve launched the Bank Term Funding Program (BTFP), providing loans with eligible collateral at face value. Following the crisis, Bitcoin climbed to a nine-month high, doubling its value from the crisis low by the end of the year. The core pattern is clear: Bitcoin often suffers losses in the "cash rush," followed by a reversal, prematurely realizing the impact of policy bailouts. Why are "rescues" structurally inevitable? This mechanism is particularly crucial now because the US financial system can no longer withstand prolonged liquidity contractions. The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) projected in February 2026 that the federal deficit would reach $1.9 trillion in fiscal year 2026, with public debt already accounting for 101% of GDP. Meanwhile, the widely followed "Buffett Indicator" shows that in early March 2026, the total US market capitalization was approximately 219% of GDP. This is a portrait of excessive financialization: sovereign debt is high, and the asset market size far exceeds the real economy. In this context, policymakers have no room to allow all liquidations to resolve themselves. The modern economy is overly reliant on asset price leverage, and governments are overly reliant on leverage in growth and market operations; pure market clearing is unsustainable. The Federal Reserve has already demonstrated this "conditioned reflex." In March 2025, the Fed slowed its balance sheet reduction; in October, it decided to stop reducing its securities holdings on December 1st; and in December, it initiated reserve management purchases to maintain ample reserve levels. Even before a full-blown crisis, the system had begun to return to loose liquidity. This is crucial: once it's understood that the financial system itself needs a liquidity restart, it becomes clear that policymakers are unlikely to stand idly by in the next private lending panic. Political factors further reinforce this trend. In September 2025, the SEC's Investor Advisory Committee pointed out that despite expanded access through a registration structure, private market assets are less transparent and riskier. Morningstar data shows that in the third quarter of 2025, the net assets of semi-liquid funds reached $493 billion. When retail and wealth management funds are packaged into illiquid credit exposure, private lending is no longer a problem for a small group of institutions, but a public issue. When opaque risks evolve into public problems, government intervention is inevitable. The Bitcoin white paper proposes a peer-to-peer electronic cash system, allowing direct transfers between transacting parties without the intermediary of financial institutions. However, the famous phrase "The Chancellor is about to bail out the banks for the second time" is inscribed in the Bitcoin genesis block (not the white paper). This distinction is crucial: the white paper provides the technical architecture, while the genesis block carries a political metaphor. Bitcoin was born out of a rejection of bailout culture, reliance on intermediaries, and discretionary bailouts. Therefore, whenever governments intervene to rescue fragile systems built on implicit leverage, the fundamental logic of Bitcoin becomes even more solidified. Meanwhile, financial infrastructure is moving towards 24/7 operation. In October 2025, the Federal Reserve announced that the Federal Funds Transfer System (Fedwire) and the National Settlement Service (NSS) would be extended to Sundays and weekday holidays, with a planned rollout in 2028-2029. This is not the popularization of Bitcoin, but it signifies that the system acknowledges an important fact: the economy is becoming more digital and continuous, increasingly incompatible with the assumptions of traditional bank operating hours. If AI agents become true economic participants, funds and collateral will need to flow at software-level speeds. This does not mean that all transactions will be settled in Bitcoin, but rather that the importance of scarce, neutral digital collateral will only increase, not decrease. Therefore, the clearest core logic is: the "tide" that Buffett spoke of is receding in the private lending sector. AI is first exposing the weakest links in credit underwriting—especially those that misjudge software revenue as permanent cash flow. Bitcoin suffered in the first wave of the shock because it still possessed both software stock beta and liquidity beta attributes. However, with high US debt, an over-financialized economy, and retail funds deeply tied to private equity assets, policymakers cannot tolerate prolonged disorderly clearing. Liquidity will eventually return. And when liquidity restarts, Bitcoin is often one of the first assets to grasp its implications. This is why private lending is crucial in the current environment. Ironically, Bitcoin was born for such a moment: a world defined by shadow banking, implicit leverage, and high government debt. Governments have limited capacity to respond to crises and can only address them by releasing more liquidity. Private lending is not just another risk exposure in the market, but rather a convergence point of five factors: outdated valuations, embedded leverage, AI disruption, retail fund bundling, and policy reflexes. Recent redemption restrictions and asset impairments suggest that this adjustment process may have already begun. If private lending becomes the starting point for the next liquidity crunch, Bitcoin's next major bull market will not begin with the halving narrative or a perfect macroeconomic backdrop. It will begin with risk exposure, followed by policy responses, and ultimately, the market realizing that the financial system still needs an injection of liquidity to function properly.

data.title: CFTC issues guidance on prediction market regulation; Iran says Trump cannot end the war.
data.description:
Headline

▌US CFTC Issues First Regulatory Guidance for Prediction Markets: Markets with High Manipulation Risk Must Communicate with Regulators Before Launch

The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has issued its first regulatory guidance on manipulation in prediction markets, requiring exchanges to proactively communicate with regulators before launching prediction markets that may pose manipulation or insider trading risks. The document, released on Thursday, is considered one of the first formal responses from US federal regulators to the rapid rise and related controversies surrounding prediction markets. Over the past year, prediction markets have experienced rapid growth in user numbers and trading volume, sparking widespread discussion about market manipulation and information asymmetry risks.

... Iran says Trump's tweets won't end the war. On March 12, local time, Iranian Supreme National Security Council Secretary Larijani, commenting on US President Trump's statement that "this war must be won quickly," pointed out that starting a war is easy, but ending it cannot be achieved with a few tweets. Larijani stated that once a war begins, its end is not easy. He also emphasized that Iran will never let the other side off easily. As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $70,534.24, a 24-hour change of +0.3%; ETH price is $2,079.35, a 24-hour change of +1.0%; BNB price is $653.00, a 24-hour change of +0.2%. SOL price is $86.92, up 0.1% in the last 24 hours; DOGE price is $0.09483, up 1.7% in the last 24 hours; XRP price is $1.39, down 0.2% in the last 24 hours; TRX price is $0.2892, down 0.6% in the last 24 hours. The price of WLFI is $0.101, a 24-hour change of -0.3%; the price of HYPE is $37.30, a 24-hour change of +2.6%.

Policy

▌SEC Chairman: Innovation Exemption for Equity Securities Tokenization Expected Soon

US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chairman Paul S. Atkins noted at an investor advisory committee meeting that the agency will vote on proposals regarding equity securities tokenization. He anticipates the SEC will soon consider an innovation exemption to facilitate limited trading of certain tokenized securities and to work towards a long-term regulatory framework. The Cryptocurrency Working Group has held numerous roundtables over the past thirteen months, met with hundreds of market participants, solicited extensive public feedback, and received a large number of written comments on how best to adapt the rules to accommodate the new type of transaction, while still welcoming input on the design of potential innovation exemption schemes.

▌PBOC: Continue to Implement Moderately Loose Monetary Policy, Increase Counter-cyclical and Cross-cyclical Adjustments

On March 12, Pan Gongsheng, Governor of the People's Bank of China (PBOC), chaired a symposium with economic and financial experts. Pan Gongsheng stated that in the next stage, the PBOC will fully implement the spirit of the Fourth Plenary Session of the 20th CPC Central Committee, the Central Economic Work Conference, and the requirements of the Government Work Report, build a scientific and sound monetary policy system, continue to implement a moderately loose monetary policy, increase counter-cyclical and cross-cyclical adjustments, and create a suitable monetary and financial environment for the sustained improvement of the economy.

... (Central Bank) The National Industrial Information Security Development Research Center issued a risk warning notice regarding the application of OpenClaw in the industrial sector on March 12th. The notice states that OpenClaw is currently being rapidly deployed in R&D, manufacturing, and operation and maintenance management within the industrial sector. However, due to its characteristics such as blurred trust boundaries, unified access through multiple channels, flexible large-scale model invocation, and persistent dual-mode memory, OpenClaw can be maliciously taken over by malicious means such as command manipulation and supply chain poisoning, leading to a series of security risks including loss of control over industrial control systems and leakage of sensitive information. Specifically, this includes risks of unauthorized access and loss of production control for industrial hosts; risks of leakage of sensitive industrial information; and risks of expanded attack surfaces and amplified attack effects for industrial enterprises. Therefore, it is recommended that industrial enterprises refer to the relevant requirements such as the "Guidelines for Cybersecurity Protection of Industrial Control Systems" and the "Administrative Measures for Security Classification and Grading of Industrial Internet," and refer to the "Six Dos and Six Don'ts" recommendations already released by the Cybersecurity Threat and Vulnerability Information Sharing Platform (NVDB) of the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, to strengthen security protection measures when deploying and applying OpenClaw, including strengthening control and access management; strengthening network boundary isolation; and properly patching vulnerabilities. (Industrial and Information Technology Security) Blockchain Applications ▌China Artificial Intelligence Industry Development Alliance: Continuously Tracking OpenClaw Security Risk Dynamics On March 12, according to the China Artificial Intelligence Industry Development Alliance, in order to prevent security risks of the OpenClaw open-source AI agent, the alliance will continue to track the security risk dynamics of OpenClaw and similar AI agents. Where conditions permit, it will promptly share risk warnings and industry security practices with member units; organize specialized technical exchanges and training activities in the field of artificial intelligence security as needed, inviting industry experts to share security protection experiences and solutions in different scenarios; compile enterprise-level OpenClaw deployment risk management guidelines, forming a self-inspection and self-test checklist to help enterprises improve their agent compliance governance system; and coordinate high-quality industry security resources to provide professional guidance and technical assistance for member units encountering problems in risk investigation and hidden danger handling. (Jinshi)

▌OpenClaw Founder Expresses Willingness to Further Develop "Lobster" with Baidu

OpenClaw founder Peter Steinberger praised the speed of Chinese AI innovation as "Amazing" in an article on the X platform, and expressed his willingness to further develop "Lobster" with Baidu. Peter Steinberger previously questioned Tencent's scraping of Skills and called on Tencent to provide support to avoid driving up server costs.

... Cryptocurrency

BlackRock's Staking Ethereum ETF to be Listed on Nasdaq

BlackRock's iShares Staked Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHB) will be listed on Nasdaq this Thursday. ETHB will hold spot ETH and stake a portion of its holdings, aiming to combine Ethereum price exposure with staking rewards while providing the operational advantages of an ETF.

Cryptocurrency

According to OnchainLens monitoring, BlackRock deposited 566.88 BTC, worth approximately $39.64 million, and 7,552 ETH, worth approximately $15.52 million, into Coinbase, and may continue to deposit more assets.

▌Michael Saylor: There is a time lag between when we buy Bitcoin and when the price of Bitcoin surges

On March 12, Strategy founder Michael Saylor posted, "You know, there is a time lag between when we buy Bitcoin and when the price of Bitcoin surges."

▌Strategy's perpetual preferred stock $STRC raised enough yesterday to purchase 2018 Bitcoins

On March 12, news broke that Strategy's perpetual preferred stock Stretch ($STRC) raised enough yesterday to purchase 2018 Bitcoins.

This amount is approximately four times the total Bitcoin network production yesterday. **Report: Bitcoin has seen an average increase of 54% in the three recorded midterm election years.** On March 12th, a Binance report showed that Bitcoin has seen an average increase of 54% in the three recorded midterm election years following the US midterm elections. **A whale withdrew 348.3 BTC from Binance, accumulating 1,720.2 BTC in the past 9 days.** On March 12th, according to on-chain analyst Ember, a whale/institution withdrew 348.3 BTC (US$24.55 million) from Binance an hour and a half ago. In the past nine days, it has accumulated a total of 1,720.2 BTC (US$120 million) through Binance, at an average price of US$69,527.

▌Grayscale Launches Avalanche Staking ETF on Nasdaq

Grayscale's Avalanche Staking ETF (ticker symbol: GAVA) began trading on Nasdaq on Wednesday, offering spot exposure to AVAX and staking rewards.

▌Whale's Mistake: 50 Million USDT Exchanged for AAVE Only Yields Approximately $36,000 in Tokens

A crypto whale, when exchanging $50 million worth of USDT for AAVE through the Aave trading interface, encountered extremely high slippage and ultimately only received 324 AAVE (approximately $36,000), resulting in a loss of nearly $50 million.

Aave Labs founder and CEO Stani Kulechov stated that the transaction was unusually large, and the trading interface had already warned of "extreme slippage risk" and required the user to confirm, but the user still confirmed and executed the transaction on the mobile device. The decentralized exchange protocol CoW Swap stated that the transaction was executed normally according to the user's signed order parameters, and no protocol vulnerabilities or malicious behavior were found; the system also provided clear price impact warnings. Kulechov said the Aave team is trying to contact the user and plans to refund approximately $600,000 in transaction fees.

▌Trump to Host Another Luncheon for Trump Token Holders at Mar-a-Lago

Trump token issuers announced that a "grand luncheon" will be held on April 25th at Trump's Mar-a-Lago estate in Florida, with Trump as the keynote speaker.

The event will invite the top 297 holders of TRUMP tokens, with 29 of them receiving a VIP tour of Mar-a-Lago. This event comes less than a year after a dinner held at the same venue for the top 220 TRUMP holders last year. The previous event drew criticism from some US lawmakers who argued that Trump might profit from the crypto industry while appointing regulators to oversee it, raising potential conflicts of interest.

▌TON Cancels Dubai Ecosystem Conference Due to Middle East Conflict

TON announced the cancellation of its Gateway Ecosystem Conference, originally scheduled for May 1-2, 2026 in Dubai, citing escalating tensions in the Middle East and security risks in the UAE. TON stated, "This was a difficult decision, but the safety and well-being of our community members always come first." The event was originally planned to bring together builders and developers of the TON ecosystem.

... Due to the US-Israeli airstrikes against Iran and Iran's missile and drone attacks on the UAE, Dubai and the surrounding area have experienced severe travel disruptions. TON stated that it is considering holding the Gateway event in other forms later this year. Important Economic Developments ▌Markets No Longer Fully Betting on a Fed Rate Cut This Year On March 12, bond traders were no longer fully pricing in the expectation that the Federal Reserve would cut interest rates in 2026. Interest rate swaps linked to the date of the Fed's policy meeting showed that traders on Thursday expected only a 24-basis-point rate cut this year, less than the 25-basis-point cut required for a full rate cut, compared to expectations of around 30 basis points on Wednesday evening. This change occurred amid a continued decline in US Treasuries, with the yield on the two-year Treasury note, which is most sensitive to changes in Fed policy, rising 4 basis points to near 3.70%. US Treasuries faced pressure this week as investors worried that the Middle East conflict would continue to push up energy prices, leading to a resurgence of inflation.

▌US Mortgage Rates Surge Biggest in 11 Months as Iran War Sparks Inflation Concerns

Golden Finance reports that on March 13, US mortgage rates saw their biggest increase in nearly a year, with inflation concerns stemming from the Iran war adding uncertainty to the upcoming spring sales season. Data released by Freddie Mac on Thursday showed that the average rate for a 30-year fixed-rate mortgage rose to 6.11% from 6% last week, the largest weekly increase since April 2025. Rates briefly dipped below 6% at the end of February, and coupled with stabilizing home prices, the market had initially hoped for a slight improvement in the spring sales season. However, if the war continues, oil prices could rise further, pushing up inflation and continuing to put upward pressure on US Treasury yields, which guide mortgage rates.

...

▌Iranian Armed Forces: Any Attack on Iranian Energy Facilities and Ports Will Be Met with a Devastating Retaliation

A spokesperson for the Central Headquarters of the Iranian Armed Forces, Khatnam Anbia, stated on March 12th local time that any attack on Iranian energy infrastructure and ports will be met with a "devastating" retaliation from Iran. The spokesperson emphasized that Iran warned the United States, Israel, and all their allies that any action against Iranian energy facilities and ports will trigger a strong counterattack from Iran. If such an incident occurs, all oil and gas facilities of the United States and its allies in the region will face the risk of being ignited and destroyed. (CCTV News)

▌US-Israel Quick Victory Plan Fails, Protracted War Under Pressure

Mojtaba Khamenei made a strong statement in his first public address since becoming Iran's new Supreme Leader on the afternoon of March 12th local time.

... The General Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces immediately issued a statement emphasizing that it would defend the achievements of the revolution, safeguard national interests and national security with a more resolute determination and spirit than ever before, and fight against the enemy's conspiracies until the very last moment. Iranian analysts pointed out that as the war continues, Iran still demonstrates its strike capabilities in missiles and drones, and possesses sufficient deterrence in the Strait of Hormuz. The US and Israel's military plan to defeat Iran with a "quick victory" has failed. If the war enters a "protracted war," the US and Israel will face greater pressure than Iran in various fields. (CCTV International News)

▌The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in March is 99.1%

According to CME's "FedWatch": The probability of the Fed cutting interest rates by 25 basis points by March is 0.9%, and the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 99.1%. The probability of the Fed cutting interest rates by a cumulative 25 basis points by April is 3.9%, the probability of keeping interest rates unchanged is 96.0%, and the probability of cutting interest rates by a cumulative 50 basis points is 0%. The probability of cutting interest rates by a cumulative 25 basis points by June is 19.5%.

Golden Encyclopedia

▌Can Bitcoin still be called "digital gold" under geopolitical crises?

Every time there is a geopolitical crisis, the price of gold rises, and the price of Bitcoin plummets. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been confirmed by data.

Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin exhibits an asymmetry: it falls with stocks but doesn't rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an overabundance of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeatable behavioral records (behavioral accumulation). Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-useful asset," capable of functioning when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries narrow, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold but a completely new "next-generation gold." Generational shifts and the widespread adoption of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.

data.title: The decade-long power struggle between the SEC and CFTC is reaching a turning point.
data.description:
For over a decade, the US crypto industry has been in a peculiar state.

The market size has grown to trillions of dollars, but a complete regulatory framework has never been established. The two most fundamental questions remain unanswered:

  • What exactly are crypto assets?

    • If problems arise, who will regulate them? These two seemingly simple questions constitute the root cause of the long-standing chaos in US cryptocurrency regulation. In the past few months, the US regulatory system has begun to give a series of new signals—these two questions are being re-answered.

      Regulatory Fog

      In the US financial regulatory system, crypto assets have always been situated between the jurisdictions of two major agencies: the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). The SEC is responsible for the securities market, while the CFTC regulates commodities and derivatives trading.

      Regulatory Fog

      The problem is that crypto assets possess both characteristics simultaneously. Some tokens have financing properties and resemble securities. Others are more like digital goods or network resources. Therefore, for many years, the US crypto industry has faced a core uncertainty: the same asset can be interpreted by two sets of regulatory logics simultaneously. This situation is referred to by many industry insiders as: "regulatory fog". Companies often find it difficult to determine which set of rules a particular product should comply with. In some cases, companies even have to deal with two regulatory agencies simultaneously. The impact of such regulatory conflicts goes beyond legal disputes. It directly affects companies' business decisions. SEC Chairman Paul Atkins acknowledged in a public speech that regulatory conflicts, duplicate registration requirements, and different rule systems have, to some extent, stifled innovation and driven some market participants to other jurisdictions. In other words, the divisions within the US regulatory system are themselves diminishing its appeal to the crypto industry. How should crypto assets be classified? For a long time, the concept of "crypto assets" did not exist in U.S. federal securities laws. Regulatory bodies typically rely on the Howey Test to determine whether an asset is a security, and this test is used to determine whether a transaction constitutes an investment contract. Simply put, if investors invest funds and primarily rely on the operational efforts of others to generate profits, then such an arrangement may be considered a security. For decades, this standard has been a core foundation of U.S. securities regulation. But when this logic is applied to crypto assets, the issue becomes more complex. Some tokens clearly possess investment attributes. Some are more like network usage credentials. And some are simply digital collectibles. In the same market, assets can be entirely different in nature. To address this complexity, the SEC proposed a new regulatory approach in November 2025. SEC Chairman Paul Atkins stated that the SEC is building a four-category token classification framework based on the Howe Test. This framework categorizes digital assets into four types: Digital Goods or Network Tokens. This classification framework also signifies that... For the first time, US regulators have systematically acknowledged that not all crypto assets are securities. But even as asset types begin to become clearer, another question remains. If certain tokens are classified as digital commodities, who ultimately has the regulatory power? In the US financial system, the primary regulatory body for commodity markets is the CFTC. This means that once certain digital assets are considered commodities, regulatory power no longer entirely rests with the SEC. This is precisely the long-standing institutional conflict between the SEC and CFTC over the past few years. Recently, this long-standing regulatory divergence has begun to show signs of easing. The SEC and CFTC announced the signing of a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU), committing to strengthen coordination in several areas, including: Crypto Asset Regulation.

      New Digital Asset Products

    • Investor Protection

    • Federal-Level Policy Framework

AI Hot Topics 1. China Artificial Intelligence Industry Development Alliance: Continuously Tracking OpenClaw Security Risk Dynamics On March 12, according to the China Artificial Intelligence Industry Development Alliance, in order to prevent security risks of OpenClaw open-source AI agents, the Alliance will continue to track the security risk dynamics of OpenClaw and similar AI agents, and, where conditions permit, promptly share risk warnings and industry security practices with member units; organize specialized technical exchanges and training activities in the field of artificial intelligence security as needed, and invite industry experts to share security protection experience and solutions in different scenarios; compile an enterprise-level OpenClaw deployment risk management guide, form a self-inspection and self-test checklist, and help enterprises improve the compliance governance system of AI agents; and coordinate high-quality security resources in the industry to provide professional guidance and technical assistance for member units in the process of risk investigation and hidden danger handling.

2. Two Nvidia Executives Join AI Investment Firm Era

Two executives from Nvidia (NVDA.O) have left the company to join a young financial firm that represents some of the world's wealthiest families investing in artificial intelligence. Geoffrey Levene, former head of Nvidia's global AI initiatives, and Max Cohen, head of North American cloud business, have both joined Era, a New York-based investment firm. Led by 30-year-old investor Jasper Lau, Era focuses primarily on venture capital while also providing a range of other financial services to clients and portfolio companies. According to sources familiar with the matter, Era is currently raising $250 million and has already secured $140 million in oversubscribed funding. Instead of raising funds from more traditional venture capital sources such as pension funds, Era is seeking funding from a group of wealthy families, some of whom are linked to powerful business figures with vested interests in global data center construction. Era also plans to work closely with Nvidia, which previously helped the company determine the priority AI markets and investment strategy for its latest fund.

... According to Era, Jensen Huang had previously helped persuade some wealthy families to adopt AI technology in their operations and support infrastructure construction, including encouraging them to invest in Era.

3. National Industrial Information Security Development Research Center: Issues Risk Warning Notice on OpenClaw Applications in the Industrial Sector

On March 12, the National Industrial Information Security Development Research Center issued a "Risk Warning Notice on OpenClaw Applications in the Industrial Sector": OpenClaw is currently being rapidly deployed in R&D, design, manufacturing, and operation and maintenance management in the industrial sector. However, due to the characteristics of OpenClaw, such as blurred trust boundaries, unified access through multiple channels, flexible invocation of large models, and persistent dual-mode memory, if there is a lack of effective access control strategies or security audit mechanisms, it may be maliciously taken over due to command inducement, supply chain poisoning, etc., causing a series of security risks such as loss of control of industrial control systems and leakage of sensitive information. Specifically, this includes the risk of unauthorized access and loss of production control of industrial hosts; the risk of leakage of sensitive industrial information; and the risk of expanded attack surface and amplified attack effects for industrial enterprises.

4. a16z: AI Agent Payment Volume Lower Than Reported Data, But Adoption Rate is Growing

On March 12, Noah Levine, a partner at a16z, published an article on the X platform stating that a previous Bloomberg article cited data from x402.org claiming that AI agents completed $24 million in payments within 30 days, but Allium Labs data shows that the transaction volume of AI agents during the same period was approximately $3 million, and after deducting inflated transactions, the actual amount was approximately $1.6 million.

... Levine stated that current AI payment activity is primarily focused on developer tools, including web crawling platform Firecrawl, AI browser Browserbase, and AI image platform Freepik. Companies like Stripe, Cloudflare, and Vercel have integrated Coinbase's x402 payment standard, and Google has also embedded the system into its proxy payment protocol. Levine noted that while $1.6 million in transaction volume isn't large, the infrastructure built around it is substantial, and these companies are betting on the market potential of AI proxies becoming the default buyer. Furthermore, Coinbase announced added support for Polygon to its x402 Facilitator, allowing developers to accept USDC payments on Polygon, Base, and Solana. Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated that AI proxy transaction volume will soon surpass that of humans.

DeFi Hot Topics

1. Grayscale Launches Avalanche Staking ETF on Nasdaq

Grayscale's Avalanche Staking ETF (ticker symbol: GAVA) began trading on Nasdaq on Wednesday, offering spot exposure to AVAX and staking rewards.

2. Pump.fun Becomes Solana's First Platform to Reach $1 Billion in Cumulative Revenue

Since its launch in early 2024, Pump.fun has accumulated approximately $1.08 billion in revenue, becoming the first platform on the Solana blockchain to surpass $1 billion in revenue.

According to DefiLlama data, the platform's revenue was $321.3 million in 2024, $664 million in 2025, and $98.3 million so far in 2026. For comparison, Jupiter, a decentralized trading aggregator in the Solana ecosystem, has accumulated $401.3 million in revenue since its launch, and Raydium, an automated market maker, has accumulated $126.9 million. Pump.fun uses almost all of its protocol revenue for PUMP token buybacks. On March 11, the platform executed a buyback of approximately $1.25 million, representing 99.93% of the previous day's revenue. Since the buyback program began, the platform has cumulatively bought back approximately $323.4 million worth of PUMP tokens, representing 28.8% of the circulating supply. Despite this, PUMP is currently trading below its initial offering price of $0.004 and is still significantly lower than its all-time high of $0.0088. In addition, public domain records show that Pump.fun has created subdomains pointing to Ethereum, Base, BSC, and Monad, and the platform has also removed the Solana geotag from its X account. Previously, Pump.fun had added support for tokens created on platforms such as Raydium and Meteora to its mobile application and acquired the cross-chain trading terminal Vyper.

3. XRP ETF has seen $1.4 billion in inflows since its listing

On March 12, according to Bloomberg analyst James Seyffart, the XRP ETF has seen $1.4 billion in inflows since its listing, primarily driven by retail investor demand.

4. Blockstream integrates Lightning Network support into its Jade hardware wallet

According to market news: Blockstream has integrated Lightning Network support into its Jade hardware wallet.

Blockstream Chief Marketing Officer Peter Bain stated, "This allows users to instantly receive Bitcoin via the Lightning Network, securely store assets in Jade-protected wallets, and transfer coins to the Bitcoin mainnet at any time." MetaDAO will launch the RNGR liquidation portal on March 17th. On March 12th, MetaDAO announced on its X platform that the RNGR liquidation snapshot will take place on March 13th at 7:00 AM (UTC+8), advising users who have deposited RNGR tokens into their wallets to be included in the snapshot. The liquidation portal will launch on the MetaDAO website on March 17th at 2:00 AM (UTC+8), and the final token net value/token quantity and portal URL will be announced before launch. Previously, the MetaDAO community had voted to approve the proposal to liquidate Ranger Finance.
